mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60d returns with Rank IC > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during late-cycle expansion show no positive correlation with INDPRO momentum (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with rising interest rates compresses profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5 and FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls signals robust economic activity and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns during payroll growth are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 2.0 percent indicates economic stagnation and weak corporate revenue prospects, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and increasing unemployment signals supply-side inefficiency and economic stress, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during post-shock recovery show no negative correlation with the INDPRO-UNRATE divergence (Rank IC > -0.02).
